perturbation.original_text: Buyer understands that Confidential Information includes, without limitation: the fact that Business is for sale; financial details; identity of suppliers and customers; and any information not generally known by public.
perturbation.changed_text: Buyer understands that Confidential Information includes, without limitation: the fact that Business is for sale; and any information not generally known by public.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'financial details; identity of suppliers and customers;' from the definition of Confidential Information, a contradiction arises. Later sections refer to the protection and non-disclosure of financial details and supplier/customer identities, but the definition now excludes them. This creates uncertainty regarding whether these specific types of information are truly protected under the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Buyer acknowledges that any Confidential Information disclosed to others may be damaging to the Business and the Seller. Buyer understands that Confidential Information includes, without limitation: the fact that Business is for sale; and any information not generally known by public. Buyer agrees not to disclos e Confidential Information to anyone other than its/his/her advisors and affiliates who both (a) have a need to know the information in connection with the Transaction ; and (b) have agreed by signing a copy of this agreement to be bound by the terms of this agreement . Buyer agrees that all copies of materials and data provided to Buyer (and any information derivative of such information) shall also be “Confidential Information ”; and all Confidential Information shall be returned to Broker in the event that Buyer decides not to pursue the Transaction . Buyer shall be legally responsible for the actions and omissions of Buyer’s advisors and affiliates.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly includes 'financial details; identity of suppliers and customers;' within the definition of Confidential Information. The changed text omits these items. The contradicted text references 'Confidential Information' without those specific exclusions, implying that financial details, suppliers, and customers are protected. Therefore, the change creates a direct contradiction because the definition in the Changed Text is now narrower than what is implied in the Contradicted Text. This aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: Buyer agrees that all copies of materials and data provided to Buyer (and any information derivative of such information) shall also be “Confidential Information ”; and all Confidential Information shall be returned to Broker in the event that Buyer decides not to pursue the Transaction .
perturbation.changed_text: Buyer agrees that all copies of materials and data provided to Buyer shall be returned to Broker in the event that Buyer decides not to pursue the Transaction .
perturbation.explanation: By removing the text: '(and any information derivative of such information) shall also be “Confidential Information ”;' a contradiction appears. The contract still refers to 'Confidential Information', however the term that explains the derivative materials should also be consider has been remove, creating an uncertainty in whether such information is subject to the same confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Buyer acknowledges that any Confidential Information disclosed to others may be damaging to the Business and the Seller. Buyer understands that Confidential Information includes, without limitation: the fact that Business is for sale; and any information not generally known by public. Buyer agrees not to disclos e Confidential Information to anyone other than its/his/her advisors and affiliates who both (a) have a need to know the information in connection with the Transaction ; and (b) have agreed by signing a copy of this agreement to be bound by the terms of this agreement . Buyer agrees that all copies of materials and data provided to Buyer shall be returned to Broker in the event that Buyer decides not to pursue the Transaction . Buyer shall be legally responsible for the actions and omissions of Buyer’s advisors and affiliates.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that derivative information is considered 'Confidential Information'. The changed text removes this clarification. The 'Contradicted Text' refers to 'Confidential Information' without specifying whether it includes derivative materials. Because the original text included derivative information in the definition and it was removed, the Changed Text creates an ambiguity in whether derivative information is now subject to the same confidentiality obligations. A reasonable reader could interpret the Changed Text to mean derivative materials are no longer subject to the agreement because the defining text was removed, thus creating a conflict with the section describing Confidential Information, even though the text is in the same location.

perturbation.original_text: Buyer shall not contact the Seller or any other individual or entity associated with Seller or the Business including, without limitation , landlords, employees, suppliers or customers except upon the prior written consent of Seller .
perturbation.changed_text: Buyer shall not contact the Seller or any other individual or entity associated with Seller or the Business except upon the prior written consent of Seller .
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'including, without limitation , landlords, employees, suppliers or customers' a contradiction is introduced. The restriction is to contact anyone associated with the business, however, the term 'landlords, employees, suppliers or customers' being specifically not to contact has been removed, causing uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: DIRECT ALL CONTACT THROUGH BROKER Buyer shall not contact the Seller or any other individual or entity associated with Seller or the Business except upon the prior written consent of Seller . All correspondence, inquiries, and offers to purchase, and other documents relating to the Business or Transaction (all of which is “Confidential Information) will be delivered solely through Broker , and all negotiations relating to the Business or Transaction will be conducted exclusively through Broker.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifically listed 'landlords, employees, suppliers or customers' as entities the buyer shall not contact without prior written consent. The changed text removes this specific list, creating uncertainty about whether contacting these entities is permitted, directly contradicting the section 'DIRECT ALL CONTACT THROUGH BROKER', which mandates all contact to be directed through the broker, including contact with any individual or entity associated with the Seller or the Business.

perturbation.original_text: All correspondence, inquiries, and offers to purchase, and other documents relating to the Business or Transaction (all of which is “Confidential Information) will be delivered solely through Broker , and all negotiations relating to the Business or Transaction will be conducted exclusively through Broker.
perturbation.changed_text: All correspondence, inquiries, and offers to purchase, and other documents relating to the Business or Transaction will be delivered solely through Broker , and all negotiations relating to the Business or Transaction will be conducted exclusively through Broker.
perturbation.explanation: The text in parenthesis '(all of which is “Confidential Information)' has been removed. The term is important because previously it was defined what Confidential Information means, and it gives context to what is allowed and not allowed. The removal of the term, create uncertainty if these items are considered to be Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: DIRECT ALL CONTACT THROUGH BROKER Buyer shall not contact the Seller or any other individual or entity associated with Seller or the Business except upon the prior written consent of Seller . All correspondence, inquiries, and offers to purchase, and other documents relating to the Business or Transaction will be delivered solely through Broker , and all negotiations relating to the Business or Transaction will be conducted exclusively through Broker.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly defines 'all correspondence, inquiries, and offers to purchase, and other documents relating to the Business or Transaction' as "Confidential Information." The changed text removes this definition. The contradicted text repeats the same phrase: 'All correspondence, inquiries, and offers to purchase, and other documents relating to the Business or Transaction.' By removing the parenthetical definition from the first instance of this phrase, there is now ambiguity in whether the second instance also refers to Confidential Information, which is critical for determining the scope of restrictions on contacting the Seller.

perturbation.original_text: In any action or proceeding, whether or not re sulting in litigation, between Buyer (or either of them) and Seller, or between Buyer (or either of them) and Broker, including any litigation to enforce any of the terms of this agreement, the prevailing party shall be entitled to recover, in addition to any damages or compensation received, its costs and expenses incurred in connection with such action or proceeding, including any reasonable attorneys ’ fees, expenses and court costs.
perturbation.changed_text: In any action or proceeding, whether or not re sulting in litigation, between Buyer (or either of them) and Seller, or between Buyer (or either of them) and Broker, including any litigation to enforce any of the terms of this agreement, the prevailing party shall be entitled to recover, in addition to any damages or compensation received, its costs and expenses incurred in connection with such action or proceeding.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the text: 'including any reasonable attorneys ’ fees, expenses and court costs', creates an uncertainty. In a lawsuit for a breach of contract, the winner will be re compensated, however the list of what can be included as cost of lawsuit, has been removed. Attorneys fees and other court costs can be very expensive, which in a lawsuit this can impact significantly to the re-compensation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ENFORCEMENT Buyer acknowledge s and agree s that any breach of any of its/his/her obligations hereunder will cause Seller and the Business irreparable harm for which Seller and the Business have no adequate remedy at law, and that Seller and the Business shall be entitled to injunctive and other equitable relief to prevent a breach or continued breach of this agreement, in addition to any other remedies Seller and Business may have at law or in equity, and that this agreement shall be specifically enforceable in accordance with its terms. Both Broker and Seller are beneficiaries of this agreement and are both entitled to enforce this agreement. In any action or proceeding, whether or not re sulting in litigation, between Buyer (or either of them) and Seller, or between Buyer (or either of them) and Broker, including any litigation to enforce any of the terms of this agreement, the prevailing party shall be entitled to recover, in addition to any damages or compensation received, its costs and expenses incurred in connection with such action or proceeding.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that the prevailing party is entitled to recover 'reasonable attorneys’ fees, expenses and court costs' as part of their costs and expenses. The changed text omits this explicit inclusion, creating an ambiguity as to whether these specific costs are recoverable. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which is the same as the original text, because it relied on the understanding that attorneys' fees and court costs were explicitly included in recoverable costs. Removing this clarity creates a conflict, uncertainty, and contradiction within the document, making it less enforceable and potentially leading to disputes.
